canonical_definition: Within the provided literature, dependency parsing is characterized as a task of linguistic analysis. The single available source defines it as the process of analyzing the grammatical structure of a sentence by "establishing directed links (dependencies) between words" (Scaling Up Temporal Domain Generalization via Temporal Experts Averaging). This task is presented as an example of one that requires a certain level of "linguistic competence". Furthermore, it is noted as a task where Large Language Models have demonstrated strong performance. The provided data does not include information on how this behavior is specifically measured or operationalized.
papers: Scaling Up Temporal Domain Generalization via Temporal Experts Averaging
Where to show Demos in Your Prompt: A Positional Bias of In-Context Learning

definitions.text: The task of analyzing the grammatical structure of a sentence and establishing directed links (dependencies) between words.
definitions.paper: Scaling Up Temporal Domain Generalization via Temporal Experts Averaging
